title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots are expected to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
NFL executives believe the Patriots will trade for Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler with declining performance over recent seasons.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round pick for A.J. Brown, with New England considering options given their WR corps needs.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' now streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is available on Netflix, continuing the zombie-themed saga directed by Nia DaCosta.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' featuring Don Henley is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeat Padres 5-2 in home opener, ending a five-game losing streak with home runs from Marcelo Mayer and Willson Contreras.
Caleb Durbin breaks an 0-for-19 slump with an RBI single, celebrated by Fenway Park fans despite early game boos.
Pitcher Sonny Gray delivers a strong performance, allowing two runs over six innings, aiding Boston to victory at Fenway Park.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots begin Phase 1 of voluntary offseason workouts on April 20, focused on conditioning and meetings.
Non-contact on-field drills are allowed during Phase 2, commencing after two weeks of initial conditioning.
Mandatory Patriots minicamp is scheduled for June 15-17, concluding the voluntary offseason program.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN mock draft projects Patriots selecting edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Howell, from Texas A&M, recorded 11.5 sacks last season and is compared to NFL's Will McDonald IV.
Jacas, from Illinois, noted for run defense and compared to Patriots' Matthew Judon, also projected to be drafted.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox secured a 5-2 victory over the Padres in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, and Willson Contreras hit a 423-foot home run.
Caleb Durbin and Marcelo Mayer contributed key hits, indicating potential improvements for the team.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover is planning a body-worn camera program for police after a shooting incident involving former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
Officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of charges in an armed confrontation, highlighting the need for body cameras in the trial.
North Andover seeks grant funding to mitigate taxpayer costs for police body cameras; $13 million granted to 144 departments statewide.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park with the Padres, acknowledging changes and new faces since his Red Sox tenure.
Bogaerts, who helped Boston win two championships, received a standing ovation from fans during Opening Day at Fenway.
Bogaerts believes 21-year-old Roman Anthony faces high expectations, having signed an eight-year, $130 million extension with Boston.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Freedom Trail in Boston offers a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracting over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England introduces the Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening April 17 for all visitors.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast runs April 9-18, featuring prix fixe menus priced at $32, $42, and $52.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and John McCauley sued neighbors over noise exceeding Warwick's 60 decibel limit since 2024.
The couple alleges harassment and unlawful noise from leased industrial property affecting their home in Warwick, R.I.
Rhode Island Supreme Court heard the appeal of the injunction limiting noise; no decision has been made yet.
